--fast-hierarchical-solution.python: Find top modules fast. Use 2 to keep all fast levels and 3 to skip the recursive part.

--verbose.python: Verbosity level on the console. 1 keeps the default output level, 2 renders -vv and so on.

--silent.python: Suppress console output. The Python API is already quiet by default; to see the engine log, use infomap.enable_log() rather than this flag. The command-line interface is unaffected.

python.name: include_self_links
python.reason: Deprecated compatibility alias. It renders no CLI flag unless false, where it maps to --no-self-links.

r.name: include_self_links
r.reason: Deprecated compatibility alias. It renders no CLI flag unless FALSE, where it maps to --no-self-links.

ts.name: help
ts.reason: Existing TypeScript argument helper exposes CLI about flags.

ts.name: version
ts.reason: Existing TypeScript argument helper exposes CLI about flags.

vocabulary.keep: First-class option on the surface in 3.0 (the default for every parameter without an entry). May carry tier: 'common' for parameters that stay real keyword parameters in the 3.0 Python signatures (#738); everything else rides the options object.
vocabulary.alias: Kept as a documented alias of another parameter (aliasOf names the target).
vocabulary.deprecate: Marked deprecated in 2.x; the surface's cleanup issue decides removal.
vocabulary.remove: Removed from the surface in 3.0, deprecated through 2.x where it is currently exposed.
vocabulary.args-only: Not a first-class option in 3.0; reachable through the raw args escape hatch on library surfaces. inertWithoutOutputDir: true marks decisions where the flag is verifiably inert via kwargs in 2.x (library mode forces noFileOutput without an output directory).
vocabulary.hide: Not exposed on the surface. Takes effect on regeneration, so it is only valid for parameters that are not currently exposed there.

python.replacement: Use Result.write_tree/write_flow_tree/write_clu (write_clu takes depth_level) or Network.write_pajek/write_state_network. The flag only acts when an output directory is passed via the raw args escape hatch.

ts.replacement: The JavaScript package runs Infomap in a single-threaded WASM worker: OpenMP scheduling, thread budgets, and multi-process trial sharding are meaningless there.

cli.replacement: Deprecated no-op since 2.x; pretty console output is always on.
Self-links are included by default; use --no-self-links to exclude them. The CLI flag is already a hard error in 2.x.

python.replacement: Self-links are included by default; pass no_self_links=True to exclude them.

r.replacement: Self-links are included by default; pass no_self_links = TRUE to exclude them.

python.replacement: Use num_threads; threads is a redundant alias of the same engine option.

r.replacement: Use num_threads; threads is a redundant alias of the same engine option.

python.replacement: The Python API is quiet by default; logging is the control. Attach handlers to logging.getLogger('infomap') (e.g. infomap.enable_log()) for the engine log.

r.replacement: Pending the R option-surface decision (#757); the library default is expected to stay quiet.

python.replacement: A DEBUG-enabled 'infomap' logger (infomap.enable_log(logging.DEBUG)) raises engine verbosity; logger levels filter the records.
A print-and-exit CLI diagnostic; run the infomap binary.

r.replacement: A print-and-exit CLI diagnostic; run the infomap binary.
